title: viola arenaria subsp. puberula
intro: Viola arenaria subsp. puberula, also known as the hairy violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Violaceae. It is a perennial plant with heart-shaped leaves and small, purple flowers, traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The hairy violet has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory ailments, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular remedy in folk medicine.
